title: croton emporiorum
intro: Croton emporiorum, also known as the Ashoka tree, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Croton emporiorum include treating menstrual disorders, fever, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or consumed internally.
